tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P1] Create config/tool-inventory.ts
tasks.description:
Extract the TOOL_INVENTORY data + types + CONFIG_KEY_MAP + TOOL_GROUPS array from `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx` (lines 31-118) into a NEW pure-data file at `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/config/tool-inventory.ts`.

**Source file**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx` (lines 31-118)
**Destination file**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/config/tool-inventory.ts` (NEW — also creates the `config/` directory)

The new file is pure data — no React imports. It exports:
- `TOOL_INVENTORY` (the const block with `core`, `tasks`, `relay`, `mesh`, `adapter` arrays)
- `ToolDomainKey` type alias
- `GlobalConfigKey` type alias
- `CONFIG_KEY_MAP` constant (the `Record<ToolDomainKey, GlobalConfigKey>`)
- `ToolGroupDef` interface
- `TOOL_GROUPS` array

Copy the constants verbatim:

```ts
/**
 * MCP tool inventory and group definitions for the Settings Tools tab.
 *
 * Display names omit the `mcp__dorkos__` prefix. The source of truth for the
 * server-side equivalent lives in `services/runtimes/claude-code/tool-filter.ts` —
 * keep the two in sync when adding/removing tools.
 *
 * @module features/settings/config/tool-inventory
 */

/** Inventory of tool names per domain — display names without `mcp__dorkos__` prefix. */
export const TOOL_INVENTORY = {
  core: ['ping', 'get_server_info', 'get_session_count', 'get_agent', 'control_ui', 'get_ui_state'],
  tasks: ['tasks_list', 'tasks_create', 'tasks_update', 'tasks_delete', 'tasks_get_run_history'],
  relay: [
    'relay_send',
    'relay_inbox',
    'relay_list_endpoints',
    'relay_register_endpoint',
    'relay_send_and_wait',
    'relay_send_async',
    'relay_unregister_endpoint',
    'relay_get_trace',
    'relay_get_metrics',
  ],
  mesh: [
    'mesh_discover',
    'mesh_register',
    'mesh_list',
    'mesh_deny',
    'mesh_unregister',
    'mesh_status',
    'mesh_inspect',
    'mesh_query_topology',
  ],
  adapter: [
    'relay_list_adapters',
    'relay_enable_adapter',
    'relay_disable_adapter',
    'relay_reload_adapters',
    'binding_list',
    'binding_create',
    'binding_delete',
    'binding_list_sessions',
    'relay_notify_user',
  ],
} as const;

/** Identifier for a toggleable tool domain. */
export type ToolDomainKey = 'tasks' | 'relay' | 'mesh' | 'adapter';

/** Server-config key that controls whether a tool domain is enabled by default. */
export type GlobalConfigKey = 'tasksTools' | 'relayTools' | 'meshTools' | 'adapterTools';

/** Maps a tool domain to its corresponding global config flag name. */
export const CONFIG_KEY_MAP: Record<ToolDomainKey, GlobalConfigKey> = {
  tasks: 'tasksTools',
  relay: 'relayTools',
  mesh: 'meshTools',
  adapter: 'adapterTools',
};

/** Display metadata for a tool group rendered in the Settings Tools tab. */
export interface ToolGroupDef {
  key: ToolDomainKey;
  label: string;
  description: string;
  tools: readonly string[];
  implicitNote?: string;
}

/** Ordered list of toggleable tool groups shown in the Settings Tools tab. */
export const TOOL_GROUPS: ToolGroupDef[] = [
  {
    key: 'tasks',
    label: 'Tasks (Scheduling)',
    description: 'Create and manage scheduled agent runs',
    tools: TOOL_INVENTORY.tasks,
  },
  {
    key: 'relay',
    label: 'Relay (Messaging)',
    description: 'Send messages, check inbox, register endpoints',
    tools: TOOL_INVENTORY.relay,
    implicitNote: 'Includes trace tools (relay_get_trace, relay_get_metrics)',
  },
  {
    key: 'mesh',
    label: 'Mesh (Discovery)',
    description: 'Discover, register, and query agents',
    tools: TOOL_INVENTORY.mesh,
  },
  {
    key: 'adapter',
    label: 'Relay Adapters',
    description: 'Manage Telegram, Slack, webhooks, and bindings',
    tools: TOOL_INVENTORY.adapter,
    implicitNote: 'Includes binding tools (binding_list, binding_create, binding_delete)',
  },
];
```

**TSDoc**: Module-level TSDoc plus exported-symbol TSDoc per `.claude/rules/documentation.md`.

**FSD compliance**: New file lives at `features/settings/config/`. Pure data — no React, no entity/feature imports. Legal under FSD.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] File `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/config/tool-inventory.ts` exists
- [ ] Exports: `TOOL_INVENTORY`, `ToolDomainKey`, `GlobalConfigKey`, `CONFIG_KEY_MAP`, `ToolGroupDef`, `TOOL_GROUPS`
- [ ] Module-level TSDoc references `services/runtimes/claude-code/tool-filter.ts` per spec §11
- [ ] Has TSDoc on each export
- [ ] No React imports
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] File is < 100 lines

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P1] Phase 1 verification — typecheck, tests, smoke
tasks.description:
Verify Phase 1 (ToolsTab refactor) leaves the build green and no behavior changed.

**Verification commands** (per spec §8.3):

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/SettingsDialog.test.tsx
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings
wc -l apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx
```

**Manual smoke test** (per spec §8.1):
- Open Settings → Tools
- Verify Core Tools row shows tool count badge with tooltip listing all 6 core tools
- Toggle each of Tasks/Relay/Mesh/Adapter switches and confirm save
- Expand Tasks group, verify SchedulerSettings renders, change Concurrent runs to 2 and confirm save
- Verify timezone select shows all 13 options
- Click 'Reset to defaults' and confirm all four switches return to enabled
- Verify ExternalMcpCard still renders below the FieldCard
- Verify no console errors

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings` exits 0
- [ ] `wc -l ToolsTab.tsx` returns < 150
- [ ] All manual smoke test scenarios pass
- [ ] No console errors when interacting with the Tools tab

tasks.activeForm: Updating ServerTab to use shared useCopyFeedback
tasks.description:
Replace the inline `useCopy` helper in `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ServerTab.tsx` with the shared `useCopyFeedback` hook.

**Source**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ServerTab.tsx` lines 73-82 contain a local `useCopy()` function that duplicates `useCopyFeedback`.

**Change**:
1. Add `import { useCopyFeedback } from '@/layers/shared/lib';` at the top of the file
2. Delete the local `useCopy()` function (lines 73-82 area)
3. Replace any call site `const [copied, copy] = useCopy();` with `const [copied, copy] = useCopyFeedback();`
4. Remove now-unused imports if any (`useState`, `useCallback`, `TIMING` may become unused — verify with typecheck)

Do NOT change any rendering or markup. This is a literal helper-swap.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] `ServerTab.tsx` no longer defines `useCopy`
- [ ] `ServerTab.tsx` imports `useCopyFeedback` from `@/layers/shared/lib`
- [ ] All previous call sites use `useCopyFeedback()` with the same destructuring shape
- [ ] No unused imports (typecheck/lint clean)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings` still passes

[settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P2] Create external-mcp/DuplicateToolWarning.tsx
tasks.description:
Extract the duplicate-tool warning block from `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ExternalMcpCard.tsx` (lines 240-254) into a NEW file at `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/external-mcp/DuplicateToolWarning.tsx`. **Source**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ExternalMcpCard.tsx` lines 240-254 **Destination**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/external-mcp/DuplicateToolWarning.tsx` (NEW — also creates the `external-mcp/` subdirectory) No props. Copy markup verbatim: ```tsx import { AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react'; /** * Warning banner shown at the top of the expanded External MCP card. * * Tells operators NOT to configure the External MCP for agents that already * run inside DorkOS — the duplicate tool names trigger an HTTP 400 from the * Anthropic API. */ export function DuplicateToolWarning() { return (

Do not configure this for agents running inside DorkOS.

DorkOS already provides tools to its managed agents internally. Adding DorkOS as an external MCP server to those same agents causes duplicate tool names — the Anthropic API will return HTTP 400 “Tool names must be unique” and all tool calls will fail. External MCP access is for agents running{' '} outside of DorkOS (standalone Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf).

); } ``` **Acceptance criteria**: - [ ] File exists in `external-mcp/` subdirectory - [ ] Exports `DuplicateToolWarning` - [ ] No props - [ ] Markup byte-identical to source - [ ] TSDoc on the component - [ ] File is < 50 lines

tasks.activeForm: Deleting old ExternalMcpCard and updating import
tasks.description:
Delete the legacy `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ExternalMcpCard.tsx` and update `ToolsTab.tsx` to import from the new path.

**Files modified**:
1. **Delete**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ExternalMcpCard.tsx`
2. **Edit**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx` — change `import { ExternalMcpCard } from './ExternalMcpCard';` to `import { ExternalMcpCard } from './external-mcp/ExternalMcpCard';`

Per spec §6.1, this is the relocation (option a) — not a re-export shim — because `ToolsTab.tsx` is the only consumer of `ExternalMcpCard` and we're touching it in this same spec. Spec §13 Q5 confirms no other module imports from internal paths under `features/settings/ui/`.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] Old `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ExternalMcpCard.tsx` no longer exists
- [ ] `ToolsTab.tsx` imports `ExternalMcpCard` from `./external-mcp/ExternalMcpCard`

**Manual smoke test** (spec §8.1 — External MCP card scenarios):

- If `authSource === 'env'`: "Environment variable" badge shows
- Toggle rate limiting on/off; change Max requests and Window seconds, verify save
- Open Setup Instructions, switch between Claude Code/Cursor/Windsurf tabs, copy each snippet
- For Claude Code tab, verify the CLI command also shows and can be copied
- Verify ServerTab still copies values to clipboard correctly (now using shared `useCopyFeedback`)
- Verify TunnelConnected still copies the URL and session ID (also uses shared `useCopyFeedback`)
- No console errors

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` exits 0

tasks.description:
Verify Phase 3 (TunnelDialog refactor) works end-to-end.

**Verification commands** (per spec §8.3):

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/TunnelDialog.test.tsx
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/TunnelConnected.test.tsx
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/TunnelConnecting.test.tsx
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/tunnel-utils.test.ts
wc -l apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/TunnelDialog.tsx
```

**Manual smoke test** (spec §8.1):
Open Remote Access from the Settings sidebar and run through the full state machine:
1. **Off → Starting → Connected → Off**: enable the toggle, verify amber dot during start, green dot when connected, then toggle off and verify it returns to gray
2. **Off → Starting → Error → Retry → Off**: simulate a failure (disconnect network or stop ngrok), verify error view appears, click retry, verify it returns to off
3. Verify disconnect/reconnect toasts appear when the tunnel goes down/up
4. Verify latency display updates while connected (look for latency ms in TunnelConnected)
5. Open passcode toggle, set a passcode, verify save
6. Verify domain field saves on blur/enter
7. No console errors

If `TunnelDialog.test.tsx` reaches into private state (per §6.7 Q5: "depends on whether tests reach into private state — they shouldn't, but verify"), update them to test through the public API instead.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
- [ ] All four tunnel test files pass
- [ ] `wc -l TunnelDialog.tsx` returns < 250
- [ ] All manual state machine scenarios pass
- [ ] No console errors

tasks.description:
Extract the Appearance panel body from `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx` (lines 212-292) into a NEW file at `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/tabs/AppearanceTab.tsx`. Per spec §6.2, the tab is a parameterless export that reads state directly from `useAppStore` and `useTheme`. **Source**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx` lines 212-292 **Destination**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/tabs/AppearanceTab.tsx` (NEW — also creates the `tabs/` subdirectory) Copy the JSX inside `` (the `
` and everything inside it). The `` STAYS in this component (per spec §6.2 — "each extracted tab is a parameterless `default` export that reads its own state…"). Note: spec says `default` export but other extracted tabs are named exports — use named export for consistency with the rest of the file (the slim SettingsDialog imports them as named). ```tsx import { FieldCard, FieldCardContent, NavigationLayoutPanelHeader, Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, SettingRow, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { useAppStore, useTheme } from '@/layers/shared/model'; import { FONT_CONFIGS, type FontFamilyKey } from '@/layers/shared/lib'; /** Appearance settings tab — theme, font family, font size. */ export function AppearanceTab() { const { theme, setTheme } = useTheme(); const { fontFamily, setFontFamily, fontSize, setFontSize, resetPreferences } = useAppStore(); return (
{ resetPreferences(); setTheme('system'); }} className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground text-xs transition-colors duration-150" > Reset to defaults } > Appearance
); } ``` **Acceptance criteria**: - [ ] File exists at `tabs/AppearanceTab.tsx` - [ ] Exports `AppearanceTab` (named, parameterless) - [ ] Reads `theme`/`setTheme` from `useTheme` - [ ] Reads `fontFamily`/`setFontFamily`/`fontSize`/`setFontSize`/`resetPreferences` from `useAppStore` - [ ] All three select elements render the same options as source - [ ] Reset button preserved with the same handler (`resetPreferences()` + `setTheme('system')`) - [ ] TSDoc on exported component - [ ] File is < 110 lines

Preferences toggleDevtools()} />

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P4] Create tabs/StatusBarTab.tsx (folds in StatusBarSettingRow)
[settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P4] Slim SettingsDialog.tsx to consume new components
tasks.description:
Rewrite `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx` to ~140 lines by consuming the four new tab components (4.2-4.4) and the new `RemoteAccessAction` (4.1). Phase 1 (task 1.5) already moved the Tools 'Reset to defaults' button into `ToolsTab.tsx` and removed the inline `` for Tools. **Source**: `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx` (current — full rewrite) **Destination**: same path, target ~140 lines **What stays** (per spec §6.2): - Function signature, props - `useState` for `activeTab` + the deep-link `useEffect` - `useQuery(['config'])` block (multiple tabs read this via prop or directly) - The `ResponsiveDialog` + `NavigationLayout` skeleton - The sidebar item list (with extension tabs) - The panel loop (with extension tabs wrapped in `Suspense`) - The `TunnelDialog` and `ServerRestartOverlay` siblings - The `RemoteAccessAction` reference (now imported) **What goes**: - The `StatusBarSettingRow` helper (now in `tabs/StatusBarTab.tsx`) - The `RemoteAccessAction` component (now imported from `./RemoteAccessAction`) - The Appearance, Preferences, StatusBar inline panel bodies (now ``, ``, ``) - The `useTheme()` call (no longer needed at this level) - All preference-related destructured fields from `useAppStore` (no longer needed) - The `queryClient` import + Tools panel reset button (moved to `ToolsTab.tsx` in Phase 1) Resulting shape per spec §6.2: ```tsx import { useState, useEffect, Suspense } from 'react'; import { Palette, Settings2, LayoutList, Server, Wrench, Cog, Bot, Radio, } from 'lucide-react'; import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'; import { useTransport, useAppStore, useSlotContributions } from '@/layers/shared/model'; import { ResponsiveDialog, ResponsiveDialogContent, ResponsiveDialogTitle, ResponsiveDialogDescription, ResponsiveDialogFullscreenToggle, NavigationLayout, NavigationLayoutDialogHeader, NavigationLayoutBody, NavigationLayoutSidebar, NavigationLayoutItem, NavigationLayoutContent, NavigationLayoutPanel, NavigationLayoutPanelHeader, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; import { ServerTab } from './ServerTab'; import { TunnelDialog } from './TunnelDialog'; import { AdvancedTab } from './AdvancedTab'; import { ServerRestartOverlay } from './ServerRestartOverlay'; import { ToolsTab } from './ToolsTab'; import { ChannelsTab } from './ChannelsTab'; import { AgentsTab } from './AgentsTab'; import { RemoteAccessAction } from './RemoteAccessAction'; import { AppearanceTab } from './tabs/AppearanceTab'; import { PreferencesTab } from './tabs/PreferencesTab'; import { StatusBarTab } from './tabs/StatusBarTab'; interface SettingsDialogProps { open: boolean; onOpenChange: (open: boolean) => void; } /** Tabbed settings dialog for appearance, behavior, and advanced options. */ export function SettingsDialog({ open, onOpenChange }: SettingsDialogProps) { const settingsInitialTab = useAppStore((s) => s.settingsInitialTab); const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState(settingsInitialTab ?? 'appearance'); const extensionTabs = useSlotContributions('settings.tabs'); const [tunnelDialogOpen, setTunnelDialogOpen] = useState(false); const [restartOverlayOpen, setRestartOverlayOpen] = useState(false); // Sync active tab when dialog opens with a pre-targeted tab useEffect(() => { if (open && settingsInitialTab) setActiveTab(settingsInitialTab); }, [open, settingsInitialTab]); const transport = useTransport(); const { data: config, isLoading } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['config'], queryFn: () => transport.getConfig(), staleTime: 30_000, enabled: open, }); return ( Settings Application settings Appearance Preferences Status Bar Server setTunnelDialogOpen(true)} /> Tools Channels Agents Advanced {extensionTabs.map((tab) => ( {tab.label} ))}
Server
Channels
Agents
Advanced setRestartOverlayOpen(true)} onRestartComplete={() => setRestartOverlayOpen(true)} />
{extensionTabs.map((tab) => { const TabComponent = tab.component; return (
{tab.label} Loading…
} > ); })} setRestartOverlayOpen(false)} /> ); } ``` **Note** per spec §6.2: the `` blocks for Server, Channels, Agents, and Advanced STAY inlined in `SettingsDialog.tsx` because their tab components (`ServerTab`/`ChannelsTab`/`AgentsTab`/`AdvancedTab`) currently return content only without their own header. Header normalization is the job of `settings-dialog-02-tabbed-primitive`. ONLY Appearance/Preferences/StatusBar/Tools render their own headers (because we extracted them this spec). **Acceptance criteria**: - [ ] `SettingsDialog.tsx` is rewritten and < 200 lines (target ~140) - [ ] No `useTheme` import or call in this file - [ ] No preference state destructuring from `useAppStore` (only `settingsInitialTab` selector remains) - [ ] No `queryClient` import (the tools reset button moved to ToolsTab in Phase 1) - [ ] Imports `AppearanceTab`, `PreferencesTab`, `StatusBarTab` from `./tabs/...` - [ ] Imports `RemoteAccessAction` from `./RemoteAccessAction` - [ ] `RemoteAccessAction` and `StatusBarSettingRow` are no longer defined in this file - [ ] All eight panels render the correct child component - [ ] Extension tabs loop preserved - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P4] Phase 4 verification — typecheck, full tests, smoke
tasks.description:
Verify Phase 4 (SettingsDialog refactor) is green.

**Verification commands** (per spec §8.3):

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test -- --run
pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/SettingsDialog.test.tsx
wc -l apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/SettingsDialog.tsx
```

**Manual smoke test** (spec §8.1):
- Open Settings → Appearance: change theme/font/size, click Reset to defaults
- Open Settings → Preferences: toggle each of the 9 switches
- Open Settings → Status Bar: toggle items, click Reset
- Open Settings → Server: verify config rows render
- Open Settings → Tools: toggle each group, expand Tasks scheduler, click Reset
- Open Settings → Channels: verify catalog renders
- Open Settings → Agents: verify default agent dropdown + runtime cards
- Open Settings → Advanced: verify logging rows + Reset/Restart buttons
- Open Remote Access from sidebar (the `RemoteAccessAction`): verify dialog opens
- **Deep-link test**: from the app, run `useAppStore.getState().openSettingsToTab('tools')` in the dev console — Settings should open with the Tools tab pre-selected

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` exits 0
- [ ] `wc -l SettingsDialog.tsx` returns < 200 (target ~140)
- [ ] All 8 manual tab smoke tests pass
- [ ] Deep-link via `openSettingsToTab` opens the correct tab
- [ ] No console errors

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P5] Run typecheck, full test suite, lint
tasks.description:
Run the full verification gate per spec §8.3 step 1-3.

**Commands**:

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test -- --run
pnpm lint
```

All three must exit with code 0. If any fail, return to the offending phase, fix the regression, and re-run this gate. Do not move on to task 5.2 until all three are green.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` exits 0
- [ ] `pnpm lint` exits 0
- [ ] No new warnings introduced (compare against baseline if present)

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P5] Verify file sizes with wc -l
tasks.description:
Verify all four target files (the new locations) are below the 300-line ceiling per spec §8.3 step 4.

**Command** (spec §8.3):

```bash
wc -l apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/{SettingsDialog,ToolsTab,TunnelDialog}.tsx \
      apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/external-mcp/ExternalMcpCard.tsx
```

Targets per spec §1:
- `SettingsDialog.tsx`: < 300 (target ~140)
- `ExternalMcpCard.tsx`: < 300 (target ~150)
- `ToolsTab.tsx`: < 300 (target ~120)
- `TunnelDialog.tsx`: < 300 (target ~180)

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] All four files report < 300 lines
- [ ] Sum of new files (target files + extracted helpers) is roughly within ~590 lines for the four targets and ~1,400 lines spread across the new helper files (per spec §1's totals table)
- [ ] No new files exceed 300 lines either

tasks.subject: [settings-dialog-01-file-splits] [P5] Final manual smoke test (all 12 scenarios)
tasks.description:
Run the complete manual smoke checklist from spec §8.1 against the running dev server. This is the final invisibility check — if the user notices anything, the refactor failed.

**Scenarios** (spec §8.1):
1. Open Settings → Appearance: change theme/font/size, click Reset to defaults — verify theme flips, font reverts
2. Open Settings → Preferences: toggle each of the 9 switches — verify each saves
3. Open Settings → Status Bar: toggle items, click Reset — verify reset restores defaults
4. Open Settings → Server: verify all config rows render (version, port, uptime, API URL, MCP endpoint, working dir, data dir, boundary, Node.js)
5. Open Settings → Tools: toggle each group (Tasks, Relay, Mesh, Adapter), expand Tasks scheduler, change Concurrent runs / Timezone / Run history, click Reset to defaults — verify all four switches return to enabled
6. Open Settings → Channels: verify catalog renders
7. Open Settings → Agents: verify default agent dropdown + runtime cards render
8. Open Settings → Advanced: verify logging rows + Reset/Restart buttons present
9. Open Remote Access (from sidebar): verify state machine progresses through landing → setup → ready → connecting → connected → off without console errors
10. Open Tools → External MCP card: expand it, generate API key, rotate, verify rate-limit changes save, verify setup snippet tabs switch correctly between Claude Code/Cursor/Windsurf, copy each snippet
11. **Mobile viewport (375px)**: switch to mobile, verify drill-in works on every tab — Appearance, Preferences, Status Bar, Server, Tools (incl. External MCP), Channels, Agents, Advanced, and the mobile-specific `RemoteAccessAction` button
12. **Deep-link test**: from any page, run `useAppStore.getState().openSettingsToTab('tools')` in the dev console — Settings should open to the Tools tab

**Acceptance criteria**:
- [ ] All 12 scenarios pass
- [ ] No console errors during any scenario
- [ ] Mobile viewport renders identically to current main
- [ ] Deep-link test opens the targeted tab
- [ ] Visual rendering is byte-identical to main (per spec §3 — "visual rendering is identical, verified by manual smoke test")
